quizzes.title: Python Fundamentals Quiz
quizzes.description: Test your understanding of Python basics, environment setup, and first programs.

questions.question: What is the correct file extension for Python scripts?
questions.explanation: Python scripts use the .py file extension.

questions.question: Which command creates a virtual environment in Python?
questions.explanation: The standard way is 'python -m venv venv' (or 'python3 -m venv venv').

questions.question: What is the output of: print('Hello' + 'World')?
questions.explanation: String concatenation with + joins without spaces.

questions.question: Which of these is a valid variable name in Python?
questions.explanation: Underscore-starting names are valid; others have syntax errors.

questions.question: What does the 'type()' function do?
questions.explanation: type() returns the data type of an object.

quizzes.title: Control Flow Quiz
quizzes.description: Test your knowledge of conditionals, loops, and logical operators.

questions.question: What does 'elif' stand for?
questions.explanation: 'elif' is short for 'else if'.

questions.question: Which loop executes at least once?
questions.explanation: Python has no built-in do-while; while/for may execute zero times.

questions.question: What does 'break' do?
questions.explanation: 'break' immediately exits the nearest enclosing loop.

questions.question: What is the result of: True and False?
questions.explanation: Logical AND returns False if any operand is False.

questions.question: Which operator is used for identity comparison?
questions.explanation: 'is' checks if two references point to the same object.

quizzes.title: Data Structures Quiz
quizzes.description: Test your understanding of lists, tuples, dictionaries, and sets.

questions.question: Which data structure is mutable?
questions.explanation: Lists are mutable; tuples, strings, and frozensets are immutable.

questions.question: How do you access the last element of a list 'arr'?
questions.explanation: Negative indices count from the end: -1 is last element.

questions.question: What does a set do?
questions.explanation: Sets store unique, unordered elements.

questions.question: Which method removes and returns the last element of a list?
questions.explanation: pop() removes and returns the last (or specified) element.

questions.question: What is the output of: dict([('a',1),('b',2)])?
questions.explanation: dict() constructor creates a dictionary from an iterable of key-value pairs.

quizzes.title: Functions & Modular Programming Quiz
quizzes.description: Test your knowledge of functions, arguments, modules, and packages.

questions.question: What keyword defines a function?
questions.explanation: Python uses 'def' to define functions.

questions.question: What does *args allow?
questions.explanation: *args collects extra positional arguments as a tuple.

questions.question: What is the purpose of __name__ == '__main__'?
options: Check if script is run directly
questions.explanation: It allows code to run only when the file is executed as a script, not when imported.

questions.question: Which statement imports a specific function from a module?
questions.explanation: 'from module import function' imports just that function.

questions.question: What is a docstring?
options: String literal as first statement in function/class/module
questions.explanation: A docstring is a string literal that appears as the first statement in a function, class, or module.

quizzes.title: Object-Oriented Programming Quiz
quizzes.description: Test your understanding of classes, objects, inheritance, and OOP concepts.

questions.question: What is the first parameter of instance methods by convention?
questions.explanation: Python uses 'self' as the first parameter of instance methods.

questions.question: What does super() do?
questions.explanation: super() returns a temporary object of the parent class to allow method calls.

questions.question: Which method is called when an object is converted to string with str()?
questions.explanation: __str__ is called by str() and print() for user-friendly string representation.

questions.question: What does a property decorator do?
questions.explanation: @property turns a method into a read-only attribute; can pair with setter.

questions.question: What is duck typing?
questions.explanation: Duck typing means object suitability is determined by presence of methods/attributes, not explicit type.

quizzes.title: Error Handling & Exceptions Quiz
quizzes.description: Test your knowledge of try-except, custom exceptions, and error handling strategies.

questions.question: Which keyword is used to catch exceptions?
questions.explanation: 'except' is used to catch and handle exceptions.

questions.question: What does 'finally' do in a try-except block?
questions.explanation: 'finally' executes whether an exception occurs or not, typically for cleanup.

questions.question: How do you raise a custom exception?
questions.explanation: Use 'raise' followed by an exception instance or class.

questions.question: What is the base class for all built-in exceptions?
questions.explanation: Most built-in exceptions inherit from 'Exception' (BaseException is the absolute root).

questions.question: What does 'else' in a try-except block do?
options: Runs if no exception was raised
questions.explanation: 'else' runs only if the try block completed without raising an exception.

quizzes.title: File I/O & Data Handling Quiz
quizzes.description: Test your ability to read/write files, work with CSV/JSON, and manage data persistence.

questions.question: Which mode opens a file for reading text?
questions.explanation: 'r' is read mode (default). 'w' write, 'a' append, 'x' exclusive creation.

questions.question: What is the safest way to open a file?
questions.explanation: 'with' statement ensures proper cleanup even if exceptions occur.

questions.question: Which module is used for JSON serialization?
questions.explanation: The 'json' module provides json.load() and json.dump().

questions.question: How do you read all lines of a file into a list?
questions.explanation: readlines() returns list with newlines; read().splitlines() removes newlines. Both are valid.

questions.question: What does 'rb' mode do?
questions.explanation: 'rb' opens file in binary read mode (for images, etc.).

quizzes.title: Modules & Packages Quiz
quizzes.description: Test your understanding of Python's module system, imports, and package structure.

questions.question: What file marks a directory as a Python package?
questions.explanation: __init__.py (can be empty) marks a directory as a package.

questions.question: What is the difference between 'import module' and 'from module import *'?
options: First imports all, second imports specific
questions.explanation: from module import * brings all names into current namespace, which can cause conflicts.

questions.question: How do you import a module from a parent package?
questions.explanation: Relative imports use dots: 'from .. import module' goes up one level.

questions.question: What does sys.path control?
questions.explanation: sys.path is a list of directories Python searches for modules.

questions.question: What is __all__ used for?
questions.explanation: __all__ in a module defines which names are exported with 'import *'.

quizzes.title: Virtual Environments & Dependencies Quiz
quizzes.description: Test your knowledge of venv, pip, requirements.txt, and dependency management.

questions.question: What command creates a virtual environment?
questions.explanation: Standard library: 'python -m venv venv'. virtualenv is third-party, conda is Anaconda.

questions.question: How do you activate a virtual environment on Linux/macOS?
questions.explanation: On POSIX systems: 'source venv/bin/activate'. On Windows: 'venv\Scripts\activate'.

questions.question: What command installs packages from requirements.txt?
questions.explanation: 'pip install -r requirements.txt' installs all listed dependencies.

questions.question: What does 'pip freeze' do?
options: Lists all installed packages with versions
questions.explanation: 'pip freeze' outputs installed packages in requirements format.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a virtual environment?
questions.explanation: Virtual environments isolate dependencies per project, avoiding conflicts.

quizzes.title: Next Steps & Resources Quiz
quizzes.description: Test your awareness of Python ecosystem resources, best practices, and learning pathways.

questions.question: Which style guide is the official Python convention?
questions.explanation: PEP 8 is the official Python style guide.

questions.question: What is a good practice for managing project dependencies?
questions.explanation: Isolate dependencies with venv and pin versions in requirements.txt.

questions.question: Which tool is commonly used for Python package publishing?
questions.explanation: twine is used to upload packages to PyPI.

questions.question: What is the recommended way to share a Python script with others?
questions.explanation: Proper packaging with pyproject.toml or setup.py makes sharing and installation easy.

questions.question: Which community resource is best for Python documentation?
questions.explanation: docs.python.org is the official Python documentation.
